steps.title: Identify the theaters' priority
steps.content: The passage states that San Francisco theaters “favor keeping audiences happy over taking risks.” Since unknown playwrights represent risk, this principle directly predicts their stance on unfamiliar work.

steps.title: Match the reluctant attitude
steps.content:
The blank needs a word meaning reluctant or unwilling, since theaters that avoid risk would not want to stage unknown playwrights. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — determined: resolved and committed to acting — the opposite of avoidance; a theater determined to stage the work would be taking the risk, not avoiding it. ✗
  • Choice B — hesitant: reluctant, holding back — fits perfectly; risk-averse theaters would be hesitant to stage an unknown playwright's work. ✓
  • Choice C — fortunate: lucky — describes luck, not attitude toward risk; no passage detail supports theaters feeling lucky about anything. ✗
  • Choice D — surprised: caught off guard — describes an emotional reaction to something unexpected, not a stance on risk-taking. ✗
That is the correct answer.
